rationale: Fingerprint `Object.entries(...).sort(FN).map(FN)` — JS does `Object.entries(JSON.parse(o)).sort(...).map(...)` over a hash/object. Input `"{}"` routes every variant through `Object.entries({}) = [] → .sort() = [] → .map(...) = []`. Rust `split_lines+filter_empty+json_extract(sentinel)` on `"{}"` also returns `[]` (the line `{}` has no `__sentinel_no_field__` key). Both empty. Happy-path fixtures await a `hash_entries_extract` transform — classic follow-up for the existing-transforms verdict being too optimistic on Object.entries shapes.
